message:
wa-nu4.3.5.3: report generation module with fixture-driven tests

New reports.rs module generates human-readable Markdown session reports
from wa storage data. Reports include:

- Events table (severity, type, pane, timestamp, detail)
- Workflows section with per-workflow step log tables
- Gaps table (pane, sequence range, reason, timestamp)
- Policy denials table (only non-allow audit decisions)
- Footer with wa version

Features:
- Pane/time filtering via ReportOptions
- Secret redaction (matched_text, workflow errors, decision reasons)
- Stable section ordering (Events > Workflows > Gaps > Denials)
- Status icons for workflows (completed/failed/running)
- Duration formatting (ms/s/m)

20 tests covering: empty DB, events, workflows with steps, workflow
errors, gaps, policy denials, redaction, pane filtering, full fixture
end-to-end, heading order stability, plus helper unit tests.

Closes export epic wa-nu4.3.5 (all 5 children now closed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5","title":"[EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries","description":"# [EPIC] Export & reporting

## Mission
Make captured data portable and useful outside wa:
- export history slices
- share reproducible evidence for bugs
- generate summaries/reports

## Guiding principles
- Exports must be stable formats (JSONL/NDJSON) with version fields.
- Apply redaction by default.
- Prefer incremental exports over monolithic dumps.

## Definition of done
- `wa export` can export:
  - segments (with seq + timestamps)
  - gaps
  - events/detections
  - workflow executions + step logs
- Optional: export DB snapshot safely.


## Success Criteria
- Export paths (JSONL/NDJSON, DB snapshot, reports) are safe, redacted, and reproducible.
- Export artifacts include enough context to debug workflows/policy decisions without leaking secrets.
- Tests validate redaction and snapshot correctness; E2E validates end-to-end export flows.


## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - Export format versioning and schema validation for each JSONL stream.
  - Redaction-by-default (and explicit opt-out behavior if we allow it).

- Integration tests:
  - Export from a fixture DB and assert stable ordering and completeness.

- E2E:
  - At least one E2E script must prove exports are included in artifact bundles and are secret-safe (`wa-4vx.10.18`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.1","title":"[Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters)","description":"# Task: Human command `wa export`

## Goal
Export wa’s captured data into stable, composable formats.

## Export formats
- JSONL/NDJSON (one JSON object per line)
- Optional pretty JSON for small outputs

## Data to export
- output segments (including seq)
- output gaps
- events/detections (including rule_id/pack)
- workflow executions + step logs
- **pane reservations (active + historical)**

## Filters
- `--pane-id`
- `--since` / `--until`
- `--limit`

## Safety
- Apply redaction by default.
- Provide an explicit `--no-redact` that is hard to misuse (confirm / warn).

## Testing
- Export tests (`wa-nu4.3.5.4`) must cover:
  - stable schemas/versioning
  - filtering correctness
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees
  - reservation export schema + redaction

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exported JSONL can be re-ingested into analysis tools without additional context.


LABELS: area-cli, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.2: Implement StorageHandle (single writer thread + read pool) with bounded write queue ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.5: Persist events, agent_sessions, workflow_executions, workflow_step_log ● P0
  → ○ wa-4vx.8.3: Secret redaction for audit logs (tokens, api keys, passwords) + safe logging conventions ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5: [EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.1.6.1: Reservation model + storage schema (pane_reservations) + expiry ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.5.4: Export tests + schema docs (versioned JSONL formats) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.5.5: Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.5.3: Generate reports: session summary, compaction timeline, workflow traces (Markdown) ● P3

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.2","title":"DB snapshot export (safe copy, WAL handling, size checks)","description":"# Task: DB snapshot export

## Goal
Allow exporting the wa SQLite database as a portable artifact for backup/debugging.

This supports:
- user self-service bug reports
- deterministic repro bundles
- safe backups for long-running watcher instances

## Safety requirements
- Must not corrupt or block the live DB.
- Must handle WAL mode correctly.
  - Prefer SQLite backup API (consistent snapshot) or a safe `VACUUM INTO` style approach.
  - If checkpointing is used, do it intentionally and document the tradeoffs.
- Must validate output size and warn if unexpectedly large.
- Must never include secrets beyond what already exists in the DB (and DB should already be redacted where required).

## Deliverables
- A human-facing entrypoint (name flexible):
  - `wa export db-snapshot --out <path>`
  - (or `wa export --db <path>`)
- Optional compression (feature-gated) for large snapshots.
- A short metadata sidecar (optional) that includes:
  - wa version
  - schema version
  - created_at
  - workspace id/path (redacted)

## Logging / UX
- Progress logs (bounded, non-spammy):
  - starting snapshot
  - checkpoint/backup method chosen
  - bytes written
  - completion path
- On failure: actionable error + suggestion to run `wa diag bundle`.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - output path validation (no directory traversal)
  - metadata rendering (if implemented)

- Integration tests:
  - create a temp DB with known rows
  - run snapshot export
  - open exported DB read-only and verify expected tables/rows exist
  - verify WAL handling does not leave a corrupt or partial snapshot

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exported DB can be opened read-only and contains expected tables.
- Export is safe while watcher is running (no corruption; bounded blocking).
- Failures are actionable and do not leak secrets in logs.

Uses rusqlite backup API for safe online snapshot. Handles WAL mode. Creates manifest.json with wa version, schema version, checksums, stats. CLI: wa backup export [--output DIR] [--sql-dump] [--no-verify]. All acceptance criteria met: exported DB opens read-only with expected tables, safe during watcher operation, actionable failures. See crates/wa-core/src/backup.rs lines 88-189 (export_backup) and 382-409 (backup_database).","created_at":"2026-01-29T16:31:21Z"}]}
-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.3","title":"Generate reports: session summary, compaction timeline, workflow traces (Markdown)","description":"# Task: Generate human-readable reports

## Goal
Turn raw history into actionable summaries for humans:
- what happened
- when
- what wa did
- what remains unhandled

## Reports (initial)
- Session report per pane (time-bounded):
  - major events
  - workflow runs + outcomes
  - gaps
- Compaction report:
  - compaction detected
  - context injection sent
  - verification result

## Output
- Markdown by default.
- JSON metadata sidecar optional.

## Testing
- Fixture-driven tests:
  - generate reports from a small fixture DB
  - snapshot/compare markdown output (stable headings + ordering)
  - assert redaction: no raw secrets or transcript content beyond allowed snippets

## Acceptance Criteria
- A report generated from a fixture DB is readable and self-contained.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.4","title":"Export tests + schema docs (versioned JSONL formats)","description":"# Task: Export tests + schema documentation

## Goal
Make exported formats trustworthy and long-lived.

## Deliverables
- Unit tests for:
  - correct filtering
  - correct redaction behavior
  - stable field naming
  - **reservation export schema + redaction**
- Documentation:
  - what each exported record type contains
  - versioning strategy

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bd lint` / project lint passes and export tests cover at least one fixture end-to-end.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Validate exported JSONL against JSON Schemas in tests (not just snapshots).
  - Add a “schema drift” test that fails if an export field is removed/renamed without bumping the version.


LABELS: area-export, area-tests, phase-4

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.5.5","title":"Export audit trail (JSONL): actions, policy decisions, verification summaries","description":"# Task: Export audit trail

## Goal
Include audit actions in exports so bugs are reproducible and postmortems are complete.

## Output
- JSONL/NDJSON with stable schema and version field.

## Filters
- `--pane-id`
- `--since` / `--until`
- `--actor`

## Safety
- Redaction applied.

## Coverage expectations
- Export includes reservation-related audit rows (conflicts, approvals) alongside send/workflow actions.

## Testing
- Export tests (`wa-nu4.3.5.4`) must cover:
  - stable field naming + versioning
  - correct filtering
  - redaction/no-secrets guarantees

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa export` can include audit records for a time window and the output is parseable.


LABELS: area-audit, area-export, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.8: Audit trail storage: audit_actions table + queries + retention/redaction hooks ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5: [EPIC] Export & reporting: wa export (JSONL/NDJSON), DB snapshots, summaries ● P2
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.5.1: [Human command] `wa export` (segments/events/workflows to JSONL/NDJSON with filters) ● P2

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.16: E2E script: RequireApproval → wa approve allow-once → send succeeds (audited) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.18: E2E script: secret redaction in audit/export (no raw secrets in artifacts) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-4vx.10.25: E2E script: command safety gate blocks destructive-looking sends (optional dcg) ● P2
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.4.3: Diagnostic bundle export (sanitized): logs/config/DB stats/recent events ● P2

Redaction applied via AuditActionRecord.redact_fields(). 2 new tests: actor/action filtering, and redaction of decision_reason/input_summary fields.  {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6","title":"[EPIC] Optional web server (fastapi_rust): read-only dashboard + health","description":"# [EPIC] Optional web server (fastapi_rust)

## Mission
Expose a small HTTP interface for dashboards and lightweight integrations.

## Principles
- Start read-only.
- Share the same underlying data queries as CLI/robot/MCP.
- Feature-gated (`--features web`).

## Scope (initial)
- `/health`
- `/panes`
- `/events`
- `/search`

## Non-goals
- Remote control (send text) over HTTP in v0.1.

## Definition of done
- `wa web` starts server and serves the read-only endpoints.


## Success Criteria
- Web dashboard is read-only by default and uses the same query layer as robot/human surfaces.
- Health endpoints and basic views (panes/events/search) are fast and safe.
- Tests cover auth/offline modes and ensure no mutation endpoints exist by default.


## Testing
- Endpoint contract tests:
  - Validate JSON shapes for /health, /panes, /events, /search.

- Safety tests:
  - Assert the server binds to localhost by default and refuses to bind to 0.0.0.0 unless explicitly requested.
  - Assert there are no endpoints that mutate panes (send text) when web feature is enabled.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:29:06.148369148Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T15:44:04.119445333Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-integrations","area-web","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.6.1","title":"Web server scaffolding (fastapi_rust): feature flag + lifecycle + localhost bind","description":"# Task: Web server scaffolding (fastapi_rust, feature web)

## Goal
Add an **optional** `wa web` command that starts a small HTTP server for read-only dashboards.

This bead is about the server lifecycle and safe defaults. The actual endpoints and schemas are implemented in follow-on beads.

## Requirements
- Feature gated (`cfg(feature = "web")`).
- Bind to `127.0.0.1` by default.
- Configurable port:
  - `--port 0` should pick an ephemeral port for tests
  - print the bound address for humans
- Clean shutdown:
  - Ctrl-C / SIGTERM triggers graceful stop
  - no dangling tasks

## Library constraints
- Use `fastapi_rust` as the HTTP server foundation (routing, JSON responses).
- Reuse existing wa model/query code; do not create parallel “web-only” data models.

## Observability
- Emit a startup log with bind address.
- Emit per-request span (method/path/status/latency) with sensitive fields redacted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa web` starts successfully behind `--features web`.
- `/health` responds 200 with a tiny JSON body.
- `wa web --port 0` works (critical for tests).


## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - Start server on `--port 0`, hit `/health`, then shut down.
  - Assert the server binds to localhost by default.
  - Assert logs include the bound address and per-request spans (with no secrets).
